channel.title: AI Thought Leader Talks (YouTube)
channel.description: Original long-form talks and interviews with AI thought leaders, filtered by Claude.

item.title: [Geoffrey Hinton] The AI Scenario Nobody Wants To Talk About | Geoffrey Hinton (The Diary Of A CEO)
item.author: The Lumen Project
item.description:
The Lumen Project · 27 min

Geoffrey Hinton, often called the Godfather of AI, explains why the technology he spent 50 years developing — neural networks — could become one of humanity's greatest risks. Topics covered: autonomous weapons, AI-powered cyber attacks, AI-designed biological viruses, election manipulation via AI echo chambers, the difficulty of regulating AI globally, lethal autonomous weapons, and the real possibility of superintelligent machines surpassing human control. Hinton argues AI development cannot be stopped and that the intelligence being built may soon outperform the intelligence that created it — raising fundamental questions not just about technology, but about humanity's future role.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] Morgan Stanley TMT Conference 2026 | Jensen Huang on AI, Compute, Tokens and the New Global Economy
item.author: Morgan Stanley
item.description:
Morgan Stanley · 43 min

NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference. Core thesis: compute creates tokens, tokens power intelligence, and intelligence translates into economic output at every level — from companies to countries. Huang explains how AI is reshaping the global economy.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] Jensen Huang: Nvidia's Future, Physical AI, Rise of the Agent, Inference Explosion, AI PR Crisis
item.author: All-In Podcast
item.description:
All-In Podcast · 66 min

Jensen Huang joins the All-In Podcast for a wide-ranging interview. Topics: acquiring Groq and the inference explosion; decision-making at the world's most valuable company; Physical AI's $50T market opportunity; AI's PR crisis and refuting doomer narratives; Anthropic's communication mistakes; revenue capacity and token allocation for employees; Karpathy's autoresearch; the agentic future; open source and global diffusion; Iran/Taiwan supply chain impact; self-driving platform; AI healthcare and robotics; OpenAI/Anthropic revenue potential; advice to young people on excelling in the AI era.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang GTC 2026 Full Keynote
item.author: Yahoo Finance
item.description:
Yahoo Finance · 140 min

NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang delivers his annual GTC keynote in San Jose, California. Covers: Blackwell architecture details, new inference chips, robotics developments, and the Groq acqui-hire agreement. Also discusses strategic partner Nebius's $27 billion deal with Meta for AI cloud capacity starting in 2027.

item.title: [Org: OpenAI Talks] Building AI for better healthcare — the OpenAI Podcast Ep. 14
item.author: OpenAI
item.description:
OpenAI · 31 min

OpenAI's Head of Health Dr. Nate Gross and Health AI Research lead Karan Singhal discuss AI in healthcare on the OpenAI Podcast. Topics: how AI models are trained on sensitive health questions in collaboration with physicians; how that foundation is unlocking a new generation of tools for patients, clinicians, and healthcare systems; key challenges deploying AI in healthcare; collaboration with hospitals; practical everyday uses of AI health assistants; the biggest wow moments during development.

item.title: [Org: Google DeepMind Talks] #64 Enformer: predicting gene expression from sequence with Žiga Avsec
item.author: the bioinformatics chat
item.description:
the bioinformatics chat · 60 min

Žiga Avsec, a DeepMind researcher and co-author of the Enformer paper, discusses with the bioinformatics chat channel: life differences between academia and industry research; the Enformer model for predicting gene expression from DNA sequence; how it builds on previous work; the potential that models like Enformer have for genomics research in the future; and a high-level discussion on modern deep learning libraries used in day-to-day development.

item.title: [Org: Meta AI Talks] Meta NeuroScientist SHOCKED Me: Scale Alone Won’t Create Consciousness!
item.author: Dr Brian Keating
item.description:
Dr Brian Keating · 51 min

David Sussillo, research scientist at Meta Reality Labs and former Google Brain researcher, explains why scaling transformers alone won't produce conscious AI — and why studying the brain may be the only way forward. Topics: how LLM + GPU may cap AI's ceiling (the lock-in problem); what Meta's neural wristband is trying to solve; Rich Sutton's Bitter Lesson and whether the field is locked into a GPU-transformer paradigm; why complexity can't be reduced upward (More is Different); and the braided memoir 'Emergence' telling his path from foster care to the frontier of neuroscience and AI research.

item.title: [Channel: Y Combinator] How AI Is Unlocking Millions Of New Builders
item.author: Y Combinator
item.description:
Y Combinator · 40 min

Mukund and Madhav Jha, founders of Emergent — an AI platform that lets anyone build and ship production-ready software — join Y Combinator's Lightcone channel. In just eight months, users have created more than 7 million apps on Emergent, with the number doubling in the last 45 days. They discuss how they built one of the most powerful AI coding agents, why they focused on non-technical users, what it's like building for a global audience from India, and the future of niche apps, solo builders, and AI agency.

item.title: [Channel: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast] 133. 对谢赛宁的7小时马拉松访谈：世界模型、逃出硅谷、AMI Labs、两次拒绝Ilya、杨立昆、李飞飞和42
item.author: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast
item.description:
Zhang Xiaojun Podcast · 406 min

A 7-hour marathon conversation with Saining Xie (谢赛宁) at a New York café after a snowstorm in February 2026. Xie, a 1990-born Chinese-Canadian academic at NYU, formerly at Google DeepMind and Meta FAIR (co-author of Diffusion Transformers, with 100k+ citations), discusses: why Silicon Valley is deeply LLM-pilled and trapped; his research taste and philosophy; declining Ilya's offer twice; his relationships with Yann LeCun and Fei-Fei Li; leaving Silicon Valley; founding AMI Labs (Advanced Machine Intelligence Labs) — 25 people, $103M seed, $3.5B implied valuation; what world models are; the future of research; and the meaning of 42.
